message:
test(fuzz): add websocket_frame_parser fuzz target for fcp-streaming WS receive path

Adds a libfuzzer harness driving the real WebSocket receive path used by
fcp-streaming over adversarial post-upgrade wire bytes. Tracks bead
flywheel_connectors-q4kuy.

Two pieces:

1. fuzz/Cargo.toml
   Registers fuzz_websocket_frame_parser as a libfuzzer bin (test=false,
   doc=false, bench=false matching every other entry in this manifest).
   No new path-dep additions — fcp-async-core and fcp-streaming were
   already present from earlier fuzz targets.

2. fuzz/fuzz_targets/websocket_frame_parser.rs (new)
   Drives the WebSocket receive path through fcp_async_core::websocket
   plus fcp_streaming::WsMessage on attacker-controlled byte streams that
   exercise:
   - arbitrary opcode / FIN / RSV / length combinations,
   - partial reads that split frame headers and payloads,
   - fragmented data/control sequences,
   - malformed close payloads and oversized control frames.

   Invariants asserted:
     1. Parser never panics on hostile wire input.
     2. Accepted messages round-trip stably through fcp-streaming::WsMessage.

   Bounds: MAX_INPUT_BYTES = 1 MiB, MAX_FRAME_SIZE_CAP = 64 KiB.

This is the third of three REVIEW-A9-FUZZ harnesses surfaced during the
swarm review pass — alongside the SSE event-stream parser
(flywheel_connectors-2qsbc, commit d25c9216) and the rate-limit header
parser (flywheel_connectors-8714g, commit ad786622).

No production source touched; harness is purely additive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -334,5 +334,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_websocket_frame_parser"
+path = "fuzz_targets/websocket_frame_parser.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
